event: Portrait of Saint Bernardino of Siena
symbols: IHS monogram in radiant disc — the Holy Name of Jesus that Bernardino popularized in his preaching
Franciscan brown habit with rope belt — his membership in the Order of Friars Minor
bishop's mitre at his feet — his refusal of episcopal office out of humility
gaunt, ascetic figure — his life of fasting and self-denial
dramatic sky — divine presence attending the saint's witness
description: A tall, narrow panel depicting a gaunt, bearded man in a brown Franciscan habit with a rope belt. He stands upright, holding a radiant disc or monstrance in his right hand that displays the letters IHS surrounded by rays of light. At his feet lies a white bishop's mitre. The background shows a dramatic cloudy sky with dark and golden tones. The figure's face is thin and ascetic, gazing upward or slightly to the side. The composition is framed within a pointed arch shape at top.
theological_description: El Greco's portrait of Saint Bernardino of Siena (1380-1444) shows the great Franciscan preacher who became famous for promoting devotion to the Holy Name of Jesus. The radiant IHS monogram he holds — an abbreviation of the Greek name of Jesus — became his hallmark as he preached across Italy, urging people to revere the Name above all names. This connects directly to Paul's Christological hymn in Philippians: 'God exalted him to the highest place and gave him the name that is above every name, that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow' (Philippians 2:9-10). The bishop's mitre at his feet testifies that Bernardino three times refused appointment as bishop, choosing the poverty and simplicity of the Franciscan rule — embodying Jesus's teaching that 'whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant' (Matthew 20:26). His Franciscan habit connects him to the tradition of Saint Francis of Assisi, who sought to follow Christ in radical poverty. Bernardino's preaching ministry echoes the biblical commission: 'How beautiful are the feet of those who bring good news!' (Romans 10:15). His devotion to the Name of Jesus fulfills the promise: 'Whatever you ask in my name, this I will do' (John 14:13).

scripture_refs.reason: The Name above every name — the direct theological foundation for Bernardino's devotion to the Holy Name
Salvation in no other name — the apostolic proclamation that Bernardino championed
Greatness through servanthood — Bernardino's refusal of the bishopric symbolized by the mitre at his feet
The beauty of those who bring good news — Bernardino as an itinerant preacher of the Gospel
